what_it_does: Describes the decide-to-SIGNAL-to-resume wire for an operator loop: a v1 implementation where every decision take is routed through territory_write to emit a decision.decided event, consumed via an API endpoint, and a v2 blocked-on registry design that is not built; it also surfaces a Tim-semantics question about what should resume.
observations: v1 is built and verified by use on 2026-06-21.
The single chokepoint is runtime/territory.py:territory_write, which writes a decision_take mark.
runtime/decision_registry.py:decision_decided_signal emits a first-class decision.decided event via suite.emit_run_record.
The signal dict is attached to the mark record as decided_signal, or decided_signal_error if emit fails.
Read endpoint is GET /api/decision/decided-signals?since=<seq>, a projection over the event log.
Autonomous resume is intentionally out of scope because posting into live channels is gated to lead/operator only.
v2 requires a gated_on declaration on work units and a resume consumer; decisions currently lack an origin/resume-target field.
A __l3_verify__ signal was left in the append-only event log as a disclosed test artifact.
Pre-existing D7 render fail is noted as unrelated.
standouts: The territory_write chokepoint is treated as the one place a decide is written.
The autonomous part of the wire is deliberately only a record emit, respecting the floor.
v2 is explicitly deferred, not built, and depends on a Tim-semantics call.
The file records a disclosed test artifact in the event log.
conventions: Markdown design doc with dated lead principle and versioned sections (v1, v2).
References exact code paths and function names.
Uses floor terminology to mark the boundary of autonomous action.
Records verification counts and test artifacts inline.
Neutral, file-local tone without ranking other approaches.
concerns: v1 leaves resume as a human/lead relay rather than an autonomous loop.
A __l3_verify__ test artifact remains in the append-only event log.
v2 lacks a concrete gated_on field or consumer design in this file.
The D7 render fail is mentioned but not explained here.
notes: This file is the design ledger for the decision signal wire: v1 is a verified event-emit/read pattern, and v2 is a deliberately unbuilt auto-resume concept pending a semantics call.
questions: What exactly is the floor and which operations it permits?
Who or what are lead/lanes in this context?
What is the D7 render fail and why is it relevant to mention?
What Tim-semantics preference will determine v2's blocked-on shape?
What is suite.mark and how is the event log structured?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it documents both the built v1 wire and the unbuilt v2 extension, and frames itself as a ledger/design substrate rather than a finished autonomous loop.
intention_signals: To pin the decision signal to a single code chokepoint, keep the autonomous surface minimal and record-only, and defer true auto-resume until the semantics of what a decision gates are resolved.
novelty: The explicit floor that separates emitting a decision signal from resuming gated work, and the practice of attaching the emitted signal (or its error) directly to the mark record.
contribution: A v1 signal contract built around territory_write, a decision.decided event schema, a read endpoint projection, and a v2 blocked-on registry concept that a fused system can keep as the documented boundary between record emission and live resume.
summary_for_embedding: A design ledger documenting the built v1 decide-to-signal-to-resume wire, its single territory_write chokepoint, decision.decided event emission, a since-based read endpoint, and a deferred v2 blocked-on registry with an unresolved Tim-semantics question.
intention_for_embedding: To record how decision takes are turned into observable signals and why autonomous resume is intentionally left to lead/operator or a future gated activation loop.
